title: The 15-Min Engine
description: Classic AMRAP. Aim for a consistent pace you can maintain for the full duration. Target 5+ rounds.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 Rounds: 200m Jog, 10 Air Squats, 10 Scapular Pushups

blocks.name: 15 Min AMRAP
blocks.instructions: As many rounds as possible in 15 mins. No scheduled rest; rest only as needed.

title: Death by 10 Metres
description: An EMOM (Every Minute on the Minute) shuttle test. This starts easy but becomes maximal effort quickly.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 3 Rounds: 30s High Knees, 30s Butt Kicks, 5 Inchworms

blocks.name: EMOM
blocks.instructions: Start clock. Min 1: 1 x 10m sprint. Min 2: 2 x 10m sprints. Continue until you cannot complete the required sprints within the minute.

title: Core Tempering
description: Focus on 'bracing' your midsection. Movement should be slow and controlled.

blocks.name: 3 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s between rounds. Focus on keeping lower back glued to the floor during Hollow Rocks.

title: The Century
description: A benchmark test of mental grit. The goal is to finish 100 burpees as fast as possible.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 5 mins joint Prep: Wrist circles, arm swings, 20 Jumping Jacks

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Go at 80% pace for the first 50, then accelerate. Target: Under 8 mins.

title: Leg Day Primer
description: High volume bodyweight leg pump. Keep rest minimal to maximise metabolic stress.

blocks.name: 4 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between rounds. Focus on full range of motion.

title: Upper Body Blitz
description: Hypertrophy focus. Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2 seconds on every rep.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 Rounds: 10 Pushups, 10 Prone Y-Raises, 10 Arm Circles

blocks.name: 3 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Rest 2 mins between rounds to allow for maximum effort on the next set.

title: The 500m Row Test
description: Pure anaerobic power. This should be a 'sprint' where you finish completely breathless.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 500m Easy Row, then 3 x 10 stroke power bursts

blocks.name: Max Effort
blocks.instructions: Secure feet tightly. Start with short, quick strokes to get the flywheel moving.

title: Desk Job Recovery
description: The antidote to sitting. Focus on breathing deeply into each stretch.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 mins light walking or jogging to warm the tissues

blocks.name: Flow
blocks.instructions: Hold each pose for the specified time. Don't force the range; let gravity do the work.

title: Standard PFT
description: The official Hyrox Physical Fitness Test. Use this to gauge your current level.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Minimal transition time between movements. Record your total time.

title: Kettlebell Swing Ladder
description: Building posterior chain power. Maintain a flat back and snap the hips aggressively.

blocks.name: EMOM 10
blocks.instructions: Perform the reps at the start of every minute. Rest for the remainder of that minute.

title: Sprint Intervals
description: Developing top-end speed and aerobic recovery. Each 400m should be at 90-95% effort.

blocks.name: 5 Sets
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between sets. Focus on staying relaxed in the face and shoulders while sprinting.

title: Pushup Pyramid
description: Volume-based conditioning for the upper body. Form over speed.

blocks.name: 1 to 10 to 1
blocks.instructions: Rest is equal to the time it took to do the reps. (e.g., 5 pushups, rest ~5-10s).

title: The 4-Min Tabata
description: High intensity, short duration. Maximum intensity is required to see benefits.

blocks.name: 20s Work / 10s Rest
blocks.instructions: 8 Rounds of 20 seconds of work followed by 10 seconds of rest.

title: Handstand Prep
description: Shoulder stability and technical positioning. Stop immediately if form breaks down.

blocks.name: 3 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Rest 2 mins between rounds. Quality is the goal here, not fatigue.

title: Burpee/Squat Ascending
description: A ladder format that challenges your pacing and leg endurance.

blocks.name: 10 min AMRAP
blocks.instructions: Round 1: 2 Reps each. Round 2: 4 Reps. Round 3: 6 Reps... continue until time is up.

title: Shoulder Health Flow
description: Prehab for athletes with stiff shoulders. Use a light resistance band.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 mins Arm Swings and Thoracic Twists

blocks.name: Flow
blocks.instructions: Move through the reps slowly. Focus on 'feeling' the scapula move on the ribcage.

title: Jump Rope Skill
description: Cardiovascular health and foot speed. Focus on staying light on the balls of your feet.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Rest 1 min between rounds. If you trip, just reset and keep going.

title: The Bear Crawl
description: Building core tension and shoulder stability through crawling patterns.

blocks.name: EMOM 10
blocks.instructions: Execute both movements within the minute. Rest for the remainder.

title: Grip Strength 101
description: Building the foundations for pullups and heavy deadlifts. Don't use straps.

blocks.name: 3 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Rest 2 mins between rounds. The goal is to reach near-failure on the hang.

title: The 1km Row Benchmark
description: A middle-distance engine test. Aim for a sub 4:00 time (Intermediate).

blocks.name: Max Effort
blocks.instructions: Try to maintain a consistent '500m split' for the entire distance.

title: Chest & Triceps
description: A classic push session built around the bench press. Control the descent on every rep - the lowering phase is where the chest work really happens.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 10 arm circles forward / 10 arm circles backward
1 light set of 15 push-ups focusing on shoulder stability

blocks.name: Main Work
blocks.instructions: Rest 90–120s between sets. Focus on a 2-second controlled descent on all pressing movements.

movements.name: Barbell or Dumbbell Bench Press
movements.note: Touch chest lightly on each rep. Do not bounce.

movements.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
movements.note: 30–45° incline. Feel the stretch at the bottom.

movements.name: Dumbbell or Cable Flyes
movements.note: Keep a slight bend in the elbow throughout. Squeeze at the top.

blocks.name: Tricep Finisher
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s between sets. Keep elbows pinned - only forearms should move.

movements.name: Dips (Bench or Bar)
movements.note: Stay upright to keep the load on the triceps.

movements.name: Overhead Tricep Extension (DB or Cable)
movements.note: Full stretch at the bottom - do not rush.

blocks.name: Shoulder Health
blocks.instructions: Light weight only. This is prehab, not a strength exercise.

movements.name: Reverse Flyes or Band Pull-Aparts
movements.note: Counterbalances the pressing work. Lead with the elbows.

title: Back & Biceps
description: A pulling session focused on building lat width and mid-back thickness. Think of your hands as hooks - pull with your elbows, not your hands.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 10 band pull-aparts or scapular wall slides
1 light set of 10 dumbbell rows each side

blocks.name: Main Work
blocks.instructions: Rest 90–120s between sets. Initiate every pull by retracting your shoulder blade first.

movements.name: Barbell Row or Dumbbell Row
movements.note: Keep torso parallel to floor. Pull elbows toward your back pockets.

movements.name: Pull-ups or Lat Pulldown
movements.note: Full dead hang at the bottom on every rep. Pull chest to bar.

movements.name: Seated Cable Row or Inverted Row
movements.note: Squeeze shoulder blades together and hold for a beat at the peak.

blocks.name: Bicep Finisher
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s between sets. No swinging - keep elbows pinned.

movements.name: Dumbbell Bicep Curls
movements.note: Supinate (rotate) the wrist at the top to fully contract the bicep.

movements.name: Hammer Curls
movements.note: Neutral grip targets the brachialis. Keep wrists straight throughout.

title: Legs: Squat & Hinge
description: A balanced lower body session covering both the anterior and posterior chain. The most important session of the week for overall strength and body composition.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 30s hip flexor stretch each side / 30s pigeon pose each side
10 glute bridges with 2s pause at the top

blocks.name: Main Work
blocks.instructions: Rest 2 mins between sets on squats. Rest 90s on accessory work.

movements.name: Barbell Back Squat or Leg Press
movements.note: Sit back and down. Drive the floor away on the way up.

movements.name: Romanian Deadlift (Barbell or Dumbbell)
movements.note: Push hips back until you feel a deep hamstring stretch. Drive hips through at the top and squeeze glutes.

movements.name: Walking Lunges or Split Squat
movements.note: Keep your front shin vertical. Do not let the knee cave inward.

blocks.name: Isolation Finisher
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s between sets. Full range of motion on both movements.

movements.name: Leg Extension or Leg Curl
movements.note: Pause for 1 second at peak contraction on each rep.

movements.name: Calf Raises
movements.note: All the way up, all the way down. Slow eccentric.

movements.name: Hanging Leg Raise or Plank
movements.note: No spinal load after heavy squats. Hip flexion only.

title: Mikko's Triangle
description: A classic erg benchmark. Pick a single calorie target and complete that amount every working minute — every minute.

blocks.name: Setup
movements: Set up a rower, SkiErg, and Assault Bike (or Echo Bike).

blocks.name: EMOM 39
blocks.instructions: Minute 1: Row. Minute 2: SkiErg. Minute 3: Assault Bike. Minute 4: Rest. Repeat this sequence until 39:00. Choose one calorie number and hit it on each working minute.

title: Boxing HIIT: Power Jabs
description: Upper body sweat with a boxing vibe. Keep your hands up, stay relaxed, and move with purpose.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 mins easy shadowboxing (light punches, light feet)

blocks.name: 10-Min Boxing HIIT
blocks.instructions: Alternate 40s work / 20s easy. Keep punches snappy, not tense.

blocks.name: Finisher
blocks.instructions: Rest 30–45s between rounds. Move with control.
movements: 3 Rounds: 10 push-ups (elevated is fine), 20 air squats, 30s shadowboxing

title: Boxing HIIT: Uppercut Burner
description: A short, punchy upper body session. Breathe, keep your shoulders down, and stay moving.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 20s side plank each side (knee down is fine)

blocks.name: 12-Min Circuit
blocks.instructions: Work 45s / rest 15s. Aim for steady effort the whole way.

title: Boxing HIIT: Round-by-Round
description: Three short rounds that build. You choose the intensity—keep it empowering, not punishing.

blocks.name: 3 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Each round is 4 mins work + 1 min rest. Keep moving—slow is allowed.

title: Boxing HIIT: Arms on Fire
description: Upper body endurance with a boxing feel. If your shoulders burn, drop the pace—not your form.

blocks.name: E2MOM 16
blocks.instructions: Every 2 mins for 16 mins: 75s work, then rest the remainder. Keep it smooth.

blocks.name: Core Finish
blocks.instructions: Rest 30s between sets.

title: Boxing HIIT: Sweat & Smile
description: A feel-good finisher session. High energy, low pressure. You’ll be glad you did it.

blocks.name: 10-Min Sweat
blocks.instructions: Work 30s / rest 15s. Stay light on your feet.

title: Cindy
description: The classic 20-minute bodyweight engine test. Smooth is fast. Keep sets crisp.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 Rounds: 6–8 pull-ups (or ring rows), 8 push-ups, 10 air squats

blocks.name: 20 Min AMRAP
blocks.instructions: As many rounds as possible in 20 mins.

title: Fran
description: A fast, brutal classic. Sprint the barbell, survive the pull-ups.

blocks.name: 21-15-9 For Time
blocks.instructions: 43/30kg suggested. Scale load to keep the thrusters unbroken or 2 sets max.

title: Helen
description: Short rounds, high output. The run is your limiter. The swings are your tax.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 Rounds: 10 KB swings (light), 5 pull-ups (or ring rows)

blocks.name: 3 Rounds For Time
blocks.instructions: Scale the swing weight so every rep is powerful and unbroken.

title: Annie
description: A pure midline and engine burner. Keep the jump rope relaxed and efficient.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 50-40-30-20-10. Double-unders and sit-ups.

title: Grace
description: Power, speed, and composure under fatigue. Every rep should be snappy.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 30 clean & jerks (60/40kg suggested). Scale so you can cycle fast singles or small sets.

title: Karen
description: Simple on paper. Mentally heavy in reality. Small sets from the start.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 150 wall balls. Target: consistent set size and short rests.

title: Jackie
description: Engine into grip and shoulders. Keep the row controlled and the thrusters smooth.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 1000m row, then 50 thrusters (20/15kg), then 30 pull-ups.

title: Nancy
description: Legs and lungs. The overhead squat demands patience and positioning.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds For Time
blocks.instructions: 15 OHS at 43/30kg suggested. Scale to stay unbroken or 2 sets.

title: Diane
description: Heavy hinge plus inverted pressing. Keep your back safe and your sets smart.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 rounds: 8 deadlifts (light), 8 pike push-ups, 8 hollow rocks

blocks.name: 21-15-9 For Time
blocks.instructions: Deadlift 102/70kg suggested. Scale both movements to keep form.

title: Isabel
description: Speed under a bar. Your best score is the fastest safe cycling you can manage.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 30 snatches (61/43kg suggested). Scale to fast singles.

title: Elizabeth
description: Fast rounds. Clean technique, stable shoulders, and short rests.

blocks.name: 21-15-9 For Time
blocks.instructions: Cleans at 61/43kg suggested. Scale dips to maintain quality reps.

title: Angie
description: A simple bodyweight chipper. Stay disciplined with sets so you don’t crater.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 100 pull-ups, 100 push-ups, 100 sit-ups, 100 air squats.

title: Barbara
description: High output rounds with enforced rest. Sprint the work, then recover with intent.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds (Time Each)
blocks.instructions: Complete each round for time. Rest exactly 3 mins between rounds.

title: Chelsea
description: A 30-minute EMOM. Keep moving, never sprint the early minutes.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: Practice: 3 rounds easy (5 pull-ups, 10 push-ups, 15 squats)

blocks.name: EMOM 30
blocks.instructions: Every minute for 30 mins: 5 pull-ups, 10 push-ups, 15 squats.

title: Fight Gone Bad (Classic)
description: A points-style benchmark. Output matters, but transitions matter more.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 1 round easy: 10 wall balls, 8 push press, 10 box step-ups

blocks.name: 3 Rounds
blocks.instructions: 1 min each station, 1 min rest between rounds. Score = total reps.

title: The Chief
description: Short windows, high pace. The only goal is consistent output across sets.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: Build: light power cleans + push press + front squats

blocks.name: 5 Cycles
blocks.instructions: Each cycle: 3 mins AMRAP, then rest 1 min. Score = total rounds across all cycles.

title: DT
description: Grip and barbell cycling. Break early to keep the bar moving.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 rounds: 6 deadlifts (light), 6 hang power cleans (light), 6 push jerks (light)

blocks.name: 5 Rounds For Time
blocks.instructions: 12 deadlifts, 9 hang power cleans, 6 push jerks (70/47.5kg suggested).

title: Kelly
description: Longer benchmark with consistent rounds. Smooth transitions win.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds For Time
blocks.instructions: 400m run, 30 box jumps, 30 wall balls.

title: Lynne
description: Max reps under fatigue. Keep strict form, track total reps honestly.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds (Not For Time)
blocks.instructions: Bench press bodyweight (or challenging load) max reps, then pull-ups max reps. Rest as needed.

title: Kalsu
description: A brutal thruster benchmark. Every minute you pay the burpee tax.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 100 thrusters. At the start of every minute: 5 burpees. Then continue thrusters with remaining time.

title: Hyrox Prep: 4x (Run + Row)
description: A simple Hyrox-style engine session. Keep transitions fast and breathing controlled.

blocks.name: 4 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Partner option: alternate full rounds. Partner A completes the full round while Partner B rests. Swap each round. Record each person’s split times if you want.

title: Sled Exposure (Light)
description: Technique first. Learn bracing, posture, and smooth drive without redlining.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 x 20m empty sled push (or incline walk)

blocks.name: Skill + Work
blocks.instructions: Record loads used. Keep every rep crisp.

title: Wall Ball Density 10
description: Hyrox-specific density test. Pick a target pace and hold it without long breaks.

blocks.name: 10 Min For Total Reps
blocks.instructions: Score = total wall balls. Keep sets small and steady.

title: Burpee Broad Jump Intervals
description: A Hyrox-style station focus. Strong hips, long jumps, and calm breathing.

blocks.name: E2MOM 16
blocks.instructions: Partner option: you-go-I-go. Partner A works for the first 60s, Partner B works for the second 60s. The team goal is smooth, consistent distance every round.

title: Carry Capacity Builder
description: Grip and posture under fatigue. Walk tall, breathe steady.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Partner option: split the carry. Run together, then alternate 25m/25m on the carry. Switch quickly - no standing around with the implement.

title: Sandbag Lunge Repeatability
description: Leg endurance with a Hyrox bias. Keep steps consistent and upright.

blocks.name: 4 Sets
blocks.instructions: Partner option: split the lunges. Run together, then alternate 25m/25m lunges (or 10 steps/10 steps). Record load and keep switchovers fast.

title: Row 2k TT
description: A classic engine benchmark used in Hyrox prep. Even splits, then empty the tank.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 8 mins row build: easy → moderate, then 3 x 10 strokes hard

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 2000m row. Record your time.

title: Ski Sub: Row + Run
description: If you don’t have a SkiErg, this mimics the same upper-body breathing demand.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Keep the row powerful, keep the run smooth.

title: Wall Balls Under Fatigue
description: Run hard enough to make wall balls feel heavy, then stay composed anyway.

blocks.name: 6 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Partner option: alternate wall balls. Run together, then Partner A does 10 wall balls while Partner B rests, then switch for the next 10. Keep the run pace honest.

title: Stations Only Circuit
description: Hyrox-style stations with minimal running. Great when space is limited.

blocks.name: 4 Rounds For Time
blocks.instructions: Partner option: split the reps. For each movement, partner A completes the first half, partner B completes the second half, then move on together. Keep transitions tight.

title: Engine Builder 30
description: Steady, aerobic, and repeatable. The goal is to finish feeling better than you started.

blocks.name: 30 Min Steady
blocks.instructions: Maintain conversational pace.

title: Sled Push Intervals (Moderate)
description: Hyrox sled push practice. Record load and quality. Smooth drive, no collapsing.

blocks.name: Work
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between efforts. Record loads.

title: Sled Pull Intervals (Moderate)
description: Hyrox sled pull practice. Keep torso tall and stride consistent.

blocks.name: Work
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between efforts. Record loads.

title: Roxzone Transitions
description: Run in, get working fast. A session built around Hyrox transitions and composure.

blocks.name: 6 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Partner option: you-go-I-go on stations. Run together. At the station, Partner A completes burpees while Partner B rests, then switch for wall balls (or alternate round-by-round).

title: Hyrox Mini AMRAP 12
description: A short Hyrox-style effort when you want intensity without a long session.

blocks.name: 12 Min AMRAP
blocks.instructions: Partner option: alternate full rounds. Partner A completes one full round while Partner B rests. Swap each round. Score = total rounds + reps as a team.

title: PFT Variant A
description: A Hyrox-style benchmark with accessible swaps. Minimal transition time.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Record total time.

title: Run Pacing Ladders
description: A pacing session that teaches you how to control effort and recover quickly.

blocks.name: Ladder
blocks.instructions: Rest 2 mins between reps. Aim for negative splits.

title: Carry + Grip Destroyer
description: Carry medley under fatigue. Your job is to keep posture tall and steps clean.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Partner option: split each carry evenly (e.g. 50m each on farmers, 100m each on sandbag, 50m each overhead). Switch quickly and keep the implement moving.

title: Wall Ball 150 Test
description: A Hyrox-flavoured benchmark. One movement. One goal. Keep moving.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 150 wall balls. Record finish time.

title: Race Sim: Half
description: A half-distance Hyrox-style simulation. Focus on steady output and smooth transitions.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Partner format: split stations, run together. Keep all running as written. At each station, split the work 50/50 (or alternate small chunks). Record total team time and keep transitions tight.

title: The Anvil Grinder
description: Elite Hyrox preparation. Heavy sled work paired with aerobic fatigue.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s between rounds. Run at 70-80% of max heart rate.

title: Olympic Complex: The Chief
description: Developing technical speed and overhead stability with the snatch.

blocks.name: EMOM 15
blocks.instructions: Use 60-70% of 1RM Snatch. Focus on vertical bar path.

title: Metabolic Stress: Row/Thruster
description: A 'couplet' designed to maximise lactate production. This will burn.

blocks.instructions: Go unbroken on the thrusters if possible. Sprint the 9 cal row.

title: Strict Strength: Press
description: Pure overhead pressing power. No help from the legs (no dip).

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 Rounds: 10 Prone Y-Raises, 10 Face Pulls, 10 Empty Bar Presses

blocks.name: 5x5
blocks.instructions: Rest 3 mins between sets. Weight should be ~85% of 1RM.

title: The 4km Row Finisher
description: Building the 'diesel' engine. Focus on rhythmic breathing and powerful leg drive.

blocks.name: Steady State
blocks.instructions: Target a pace 10-15 seconds slower than your 2k split pace.

title: Front Squat Volume
description: High density strength work. Short rest periods build capacity under fatigue.

blocks.name: 10x3
blocks.instructions: Perform 3 reps every 90 seconds. Weight should be 80% of 1RM.

title: Murph (Unpartitioned)
description: The gold standard of endurance. No partitioning means finishing all reps of one movement before the next.

blocks.name: For Time (20lb Vest)
blocks.instructions: Complete in order: 1 Mile Run, then all Pullups, then all Pushups, then all Squats, then 1 Mile Run.

title: The Sandbag Mile
description: Brutal core and carry endurance. Use a shoulder or 'bear hug' carry.

blocks.name: 1600m Carry
blocks.instructions: Cover 1 mile as fast as possible. Switch carrying positions as needed.

title: Pistol Squat Skill
description: Unilateral strength and balance. Use a counterweight or bench for support if needed.

blocks.name: EMOM 10
blocks.instructions: Perform 3-5 pistols per leg every minute. Rest remainder of minute.

title: Double Under Mastery
description: Efficiency test. Double unders require a relaxed grip and high vertical jumps.

blocks.name: 10 min EMOM
blocks.instructions: Accumulate 30-50 reps per minute. Focus on timing, not speed.

title: Heavy Grace
description: 30 Clean and Jerks for time. This is a classic CrossFit benchmark at a pro weight.

blocks.name: 30 Reps
blocks.instructions: Weight: 61/43kg. Target is sub 5:00 for elite athletes.

title: The Ergathlon
description: Triple-erg test. Minimal transitions are key. Maintain a steady aerobic pace.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 mins on each erg at low intensity

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Move quickly between machines. Do not stop moving.

title: Pause Squat Intensity
description: Removing the 'stretch reflex' to build raw strength from the bottom of the squat.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: Hip openers, 2 sets of 10 squats (Empty bar)

blocks.name: 6x2
blocks.instructions: Hold for 3 seconds in the bottom. Rest 3 mins between sets.

title: Burpee/Box Jump 50
description: High-volume plyometrics and metabolic demand. Stay efficient on the box step-down.

blocks.instructions: Descending rep scheme. Rest only as needed to keep moving.

title: Snatch Balance: Stability
description: A technical drill to build confidence in 'catching' the snatch in a deep overhead squat.

blocks.name: 5x3
blocks.instructions: Moderate weight. Focus on the 'punch' under the bar.

title: Muscle-Up Progressions
description: Elite gymnastics skill work. Focus on the 'transition' through the rings/bar.

blocks.name: EMOM 12
blocks.instructions: Perform 3-5 reps or the hardest progression you can master.

title: The 5km Run Benchmark
description: Aerobic capacity test. Aim for a sub 20:00 (Advanced) or sub 18:00 (Elite).

blocks.name: Time Trial
blocks.instructions: Run on a flat course or track. Pacing is crucial in the first 2km.

title: Devil Press Ladder
description: Extremely high metabolic demand. Use the swing of the dumbbells to help the overhead movement.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Descending ladder of Devil Press (10 to 1) and Box Step Ups (20 to 2).

title: Weighted Dips / Pullups
description: Old school strength for upper body mass and power. Use a dipping belt.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 sets of bodyweight pullups and dips

blocks.name: 5x5
blocks.instructions: Superset movements. Rest 2-3 mins between sets.

title: The Hybrid Finisher
description: A combination of erg and bodyweight movements to finish the week.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: No rest between stations. Push the pace on the 500m runs.

title: Chest Day
description: Full chest and tricep session with structured progressive loading. The pec deck finisher will leave you unable to tie your own shoelaces.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 1 x 15 empty-bar or very light bench press - focus on bar path

blocks.name: Primary Compound
blocks.instructions: Rest 2–3 mins between sets. Controlled 2s descent on every rep.

movements.name: Barbell Bench Press
movements.note: Touch lower chest. Leg drive throughout. No bouncing.

blocks.name: Secondary Compound
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between sets. Feel the upper chest stretch on the way down.

movements.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
movements.note: 30–45° incline. Allow dumbbells to travel below chest level for maximum stretch.

movements.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
movements.note: No stretch reflex - pure tricep and inner chest tension from a dead stop.

blocks.name: Isolation
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s between sets. No lockout - keep constant tension on the pecs.

movements.name: Pec Deck or Cable Flyes
movements.note: Pause for 1 second at peak contraction. Think about wrapping arms around a barrel.

movements.name: Cable Crossover (High to Low)
movements.note: Targets the lower chest. Keep a slight forward lean and don't fully extend the elbows.

blocks.name: Tricep Finisher
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s. Elbows stay pinned throughout.

movements.name: Skull Crushers
movements.note: Elbows pointing at the ceiling. Focus on the stretch at the bottom.

movements.name: Tricep Pushdowns (Cable)
movements.note: Full extension at the bottom. Squeeze the lateral head hard.

blocks.name: Posterior Health
blocks.instructions: Light weight only. Essential after a heavy pressing session.

movements.name: Reverse Flyes or Face Pulls
movements.note: Protects the rotator cuff over time. Never skip this.

title: Back Day
description: A complete back session targeting thickness, width, and rear delt health. Pull with your elbows - your hands are just hooks.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 1 x 10 light dumbbell rows each side - focus on scapular retraction

blocks.name: Primary Compound
blocks.instructions: Rest 2–3 mins between sets. Torso parallel to floor. Pull elbows into back pockets.

movements.name: Barbell Row
movements.note: Heavy. Reset each rep. Build the mid-back thickness that defines a serious physique.

blocks.name: Vertical Pull
blocks.instructions: Rest 2 mins between sets. Full dead hang at the bottom - every rep.

movements.name: Weighted Pull-ups or Lat Pulldown
movements.note: Initiate by depressing the shoulder blade before bending the elbow. Pull chest to bar.

movements.name: Single Arm Lat Pulldown
movements.note: Allows you to focus on each lat independently. Full stretch at the top.

blocks.name: Horizontal Pull
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between sets.

movements.name: Seated Cable Row
movements.note: Slight forward lean at start. Sit tall at peak. Hold the contraction for a beat before releasing.

movements.name: T-Bar Row
movements.note: Chest on the pad. Neutral grip. Pull elbows past your torso - this is where the mid-back really fires.

blocks.name: Lat Isolation
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s. Arms stay straight - this is not a row.

movements.name: Straight Arm Pulldown
movements.note: Pull the shoulder blades down and feel the lats contract fully at the bottom.

movements.name: Sit-ups
movements.note: Controlled reps. Exhale as you sit up, keep your chin tucked, and don't pull on the neck. Aim for consistent tempo rather than speed.

title: Legs Day
description: The most demanding session of the week. Quad, hamstring, and glute work across every major movement pattern. You earn the rest days after this one.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 5 mins bike - thorough warm-up for knees and hips
30s hip flexor stretch each side / 30s pigeon pose each side
10 glute bridges with 2s pause at the top
1 x 10 goblet squats focusing on depth

blocks.name: Primary Compound
blocks.instructions: Rest 2.5–3 mins between sets. This is the most important set of the week - earn it.

movements.name: Barbell Back Squat
movements.note: Sit back and down. Hip crease below knee. Drive the floor away - do not just stand up.

blocks.name: Quad Focus
blocks.instructions: Rest 2 mins between sets.

movements.name: Hack Squat or Leg Press
movements.note: Hack squat: constant tension, no lockout. Leg press: feet mid-platform, do not let lower back peel off the seat.

movements.name: Leg Extension
movements.note: Pause for 1 full second at the top. Control the eccentric all the way down.

blocks.name: Posterior Chain
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between sets.

movements.name: Romanian Deadlift
movements.note: Push hips back until deep hamstring stretch. Drive hips through at the top and squeeze glutes hard.

movements.name: Hip Thrust (Barbell)
movements.note: Use a pad. Squeeze glutes for 1 full second at the top. Shins vertical at peak contraction.

movements.name: Lying or Seated Leg Curl
movements.note: Slow eccentric. Do not let the stack slam at the bottom - maintain hamstring tension throughout.

blocks.name: Calves
blocks.instructions: Rest 45s between sets. Full range of motion - all the way up, all the way down.

movements.name: Calf Raises (Machine or Standing)
movements.note: Pause at the top. Calves respond well to high volume and slow eccentrics.

movements.name: Hanging Leg Raise
movements.note: No spinal flexion load after heavy squats. Hip flexion only. Avoid swinging.

title: Shoulders Day
description: Full shoulder development from pressing strength to lateral width and rear delt health. The face pull at the end is not optional.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 10 arm circles forward / 10 arm circles backward
1 x 10 empty-bar overhead press - focus on lockout

blocks.name: Primary Press
blocks.instructions: Rest 2–3 mins between sets. No leg drive - strict press only.

movements.name: Barbell Overhead Press
movements.note: Squeeze glutes and brace core. Press the bar vertically. Head through the window at the top.

blocks.name: Secondary Press
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between sets.

movements.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Arnold Press
movements.note: Arnold press: rotate from palms-in to palms-forward through the press. Recruits all three delt heads.

movements.name: Machine Shoulder Press
movements.note: Eliminates stability demands - focus entirely on deltoid recruitment. Control the eccentric.

blocks.name: Lateral Development
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s. Lead with the elbows - think about touching the side walls.

movements.name: Lateral Raises (Dumbbell or Cable)
movements.note: Use lighter weight than feels necessary. Stop slightly above shoulder height.

movements.name: Upright Row (Cable or Barbell)
movements.note: Wide grip to reduce shoulder impingement risk. Elbows lead the movement.

blocks.name: Rear Delt & Health
blocks.instructions: Light weight. This is the most important part of the session for long-term shoulder health.

movements.name: Face Pulls (Cable)
movements.note: Pull to forehead. Elbows flared high. Emphasise the external rotation at the end of every rep.

movements.name: Reverse Flyes (Dumbbell or Machine)
movements.note: Focus on feeling the rear delt contract - not the traps.

movements.name: Russian Twists
movements.note: Feet off the floor. Rotate from the obliques. Add a plate in later weeks.

title: Arms Day
description: Dedicated bicep and tricep session. The arms respond exceptionally well to focused isolation work - give them your full attention for once.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 30s wrist flexor stretch each side / 30s wrist extensor stretch each side
10 arm circles forward / 10 arm circles backward
1 x 15 cable curls (light) / 1 x 15 tricep pushdowns (light) - pump blood into the elbow joints

blocks.name: Biceps - Compound
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between sets. Elbows stay pinned - no swinging.

movements.name: Barbell or EZ Bar Curl
movements.note: The heaviest bicep movement of the session. Full extension at the bottom - do not cheat the range.

movements.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
movements.note: The incline position stretches the long head of the bicep maximally. Slow and deliberate on every rep.

blocks.name: Biceps - Isolation
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s. No momentum - the bicep does all the work.

movements.name: Preacher Curl (Barbell or EZ Bar)
movements.note: The bench eliminates all cheating. Resist the urge to bounce at the bottom - that stretch is where growth happens.

movements.name: Hammer Curls
movements.note: Neutral grip targets the brachialis - developing this pushes the bicep peak higher.

movements.name: Cable Curl (Single Arm)
movements.note: Constant tension from the cable. Supinate hard at the top of each rep.

blocks.name: Triceps - Compound
blocks.instructions: Rest 90s between sets. Elbows stay tucked throughout.

movements.name: Skull Crushers (Barbell or EZ Bar)
movements.note: Lower to forehead with elbows pointing at the ceiling. The long head stretch at the bottom is where size comes from.

movements.name: Overhead Tricep Extension (Cable or Dumbbell)
movements.note: Overhead position places the long head in full stretch. Keep elbows pointing straight up - do not let them flare.

blocks.name: Triceps - Isolation
blocks.instructions: Rest 60s. Squeeze the lateral head hard on every rep.

movements.name: Tricep Pushdowns (Cable - Straight Bar or Rope)
movements.note: Full extension at the bottom. Rope allows a twist at the bottom to maximise lateral head contraction.

movements.name: Dips (Upright / Tricep Focus)
movements.note: Stay upright to keep the load on the triceps. Excellent mass-builder to close out the session.

blocks.name: Forearm Finisher
blocks.instructions: Light weight, high rep. Grip strength underpins every compound lift you do.

movements.name: Hammer Curls (Slow)
movements.note: Focus on the forearm and brachioradialis at the top. Full extension at the bottom.

title: Eva
description: A high-volume benchmark. Long rounds, constant grip demand, and a steady run pace.

blocks.name: 5 Rounds For Time
blocks.instructions: Keep the run smooth and the swings powerful. Break pull-ups before you fail reps.

title: King Kong
description: A heavy classic. Only for athletes with solid technique and strict standards.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: Build to moderate heavy deadlift + squat clean

blocks.name: 3 Rounds For Time
blocks.instructions: Scale loads and/or skills to keep reps safe and clean.

title: Nate
description: A 20-minute AMRAP that rewards efficient gymnastics and a strong hinge.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: Skill: 5–10 mins muscle-up practice (or strict pull-up + dip)

blocks.name: 20 Min AMRAP
blocks.instructions: Score = rounds + reps. Stay efficient on transitions.

title: Randy
description: A fast barbell benchmark. Crisp reps only. Grip and lungs will be tested.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: 75 power snatches (34/25kg suggested). Scale to safe cycling.

title: Amanda
description: A sharp classic: high-skill and fast reps. Scale to maintain standards.

blocks.name: 9-7-5 For Time
blocks.instructions: Muscle-ups + snatches (heavy). Scale both to keep reps safe.

title: Race Sim: Full
description: A full Hyrox-style simulation. Long, demanding, and specific. Keep moving.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Partner format: split stations, run together. Keep all running as written. At each station, split the work 50/50 (or alternate small chunks). Record total team time. Scale stations if needed, but keep the run volume.

title: Sled Heavy Day
description: Hyrox sled day. Heavy exposure with enough aerobic work to keep you honest.

blocks.name: Warmup
movements: 2 x 20m light sled push, 2 x 20m light sled pull

blocks.name: Work
blocks.instructions: Partner format: alternate efforts. Partner A completes one sled push + one sled pull while Partner B rests, then swap. Run reps together at steady pace. Record loads used.

title: Elite Stations Repeat
description: High output station repeats. Big breathing. Tight transitions.

blocks.name: 4 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Partner format: split stations, run together. For each station, split reps/distance 50/50. Rest 2 mins between rounds. Keep intensity high but repeatable.

title: Hyrox PFT (Pro Standard)
description: A higher-standard Hyrox fitness test. Record finish time and station notes.

blocks.name: For Time
blocks.instructions: Minimal transition time. Record total time.

title: Roxzone Killer
description: Shorter runs, brutal transitions, dense stations. Designed to make you better at the messy middle.

blocks.name: 8 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Partner format: alternate rounds. Partner A completes one full round while Partner B rests, then swap next round. Keep transitions fast and form clean.

title: Order 66
description: A high-output Hyrox-style workout chipper, designed in homage to the iconic Star Wars films.

blocks.name: 8 Rounds
blocks.instructions: Partner format: alternate as you please, one rests the other works.

title: Spine Basics
description: A gentle introduction to Pilates fundamentals. Breathing, neutral spine, and the imprinted position. If you've never done Pilates, start here.

blocks.name: Foundation
blocks.instructions: Move slowly. Find neutral spine before every exercise. Exhale on the effort.
movements: Single Knee Folds (5 each side, slow)
Shoulder Bridge (8 reps - no weight, 2s pause at top)

title: Core Awakening
description: Targeted deep core activation. Transverse abdominis, pelvic floor, and diaphragm working as a unit. The foundation everything else is built on.

blocks.name: Activation Sequence
blocks.instructions: Never hold your breath. Think 30% effort on the deep core - not a brace, a gentle tension.
movements: Supine Breathing into the pelvic floor (10 breaths)
Dead Bug - arm only (8 each side)
Dead Bug - leg only (8 each side)
Dead Bug - opposite arm and leg (6 each side)

blocks.name: Finisher
blocks.instructions: Slow and deliberate. Quality over quantity.

title: Morning Mobility
description: Spine mobility and hip opening for the morning. Pairs well with strength training days. No equipment, no sweat required.

blocks.name: Spine Mobility
blocks.instructions: Move through the full range available to you today. No forcing.
movements: Child's Pose with Lateral Reach (30s each side)

blocks.name: Hip Opening
blocks.instructions: Breathe into restriction. Let the exhale deepen the stretch.
movements: Glute Bridge with Hip Circle (8 each direction)

title: Roll & Release
description: Spine articulation and hamstring lengthening. This is the session that makes everything else feel easier.

blocks.name: Articulation
blocks.instructions: Every vertebra moves. If a segment is stiff, breathe into it and slow down.
movements: Roll Up (6 reps - controlled, no momentum)

blocks.name: Lengthening
blocks.instructions: Reach through the heels. Keep the core switched on.
movements: Single Leg Hamstring Stretch (45s each side)

title: Posture Reset
description: Desk-life antidote. Thoracic extension, scapular stability, and hip flexor release. Do this after every long sitting day.

blocks.name: Thoracic Mobility
blocks.instructions: Focus on the upper and mid back - not the lower back. Keep the lumbar still.
movements: Thoracic Extension over Foam Roller (6 segments, 30s each)

blocks.name: Scapular Control
blocks.instructions: Think about the shoulder blades moving on the ribcage like a drawer.

blocks.name: Hip Flexor Release
blocks.instructions: Hold the posterior pelvic tilt throughout. Do not arch the lower back.
movements: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch with Reach (45s each side)

title: The Hundred
description: The benchmark Pilates endurance drill. One hundred beats of the arms while holding a curl-up. Simple. Brutal. Effective.

blocks.name: Preparation
blocks.instructions: Build up the curl-up height over the first few sets. Knees bent is fine.

blocks.name: The Hundred
blocks.instructions: Inhale for 5 beats, exhale for 5 beats. Arms pump 2-3 inches. Hold the position - do not pulse the spine.
movements: The Hundred (100 beats total - 10 breath cycles)

title: Hip Stability Flow
description: Glute medius, hip rotators, and lateral hip chain. The stability work that protects your knees and lower back during every compound lift.

blocks.name: Activation
blocks.instructions: Feel the target muscle - not the TFL or lower back. Slow and controlled.
movements: Side-Lying Hip Circle (8 each direction, each side)

blocks.name: Loading
blocks.instructions: Add a resistance band above the knees on all movements if available.
movements: Lateral Band Walk (20 steps each direction)
Single Leg Glute Bridge (10 each side, 2s hold at top)
Donkey Kick into Fire Hydrant (10 each side)

blocks.name: Integration
blocks.instructions: Combine the hip stability with spinal control.
movements: Bird Dog with Hip Hike (8 each side)
Side Plank Hip Dip (10 each side)

title: Athletic Core
description: Rotational power and anti-rotation stability. The core work that actually translates to lifting and sport.

blocks.name: Anti-Rotation
blocks.instructions: Keep the pelvis completely still during all rotation patterns. The movement comes from the thorax.
movements: Dead Bug (10 each side - slow, 3s lower)
Pallof Press Hold (3 x 20s each side)

blocks.name: Rotational Power
blocks.instructions: Control the deceleration. Power through the rotation, resist on the return.
movements: Seated Rotation with Reach (12 each side)

blocks.name: Endurance
blocks.instructions: Hold quality throughout. Do not sacrifice depth for speed.
movements: Side Plank with Hip Abduction (10 each side)

title: Spine Articulation Deep Dive
description: Full spinal mobility through every plane. Flexion, extension, lateral flexion, and rotation. The session that restores what heavy lifting takes away.

blocks.name: Flexion and Extension
blocks.instructions: Segment by segment. Find the stiff spots and breathe into them.
movements: Swan Prep (8 reps - thoracic extension only)
Full Swan (5 reps - add lumbar extension gradually)

blocks.name: Lateral and Rotation
blocks.instructions: Keep the pelvis level. The movement is in the spine, not the hips.

blocks.name: Full Integration
blocks.instructions: Combine patterns. Move with intention.
movements: Spine Stretch Forward into Swan (6 full reps)

title: Powerhouse Session
description: Pilates powerhouse = core, glutes, inner thighs, and pelvic floor as a unit. This is where real Pilates strength lives.

blocks.name: Pelvic Control
blocks.instructions: Posterior pelvic tilt is your home base. Return to it between every movement.
movements: Shoulder Bridge with Leg Extension (6 each side)

blocks.name: Deep Core Series
blocks.instructions: Exhale drives the contraction. Never grip through the hip flexors.

blocks.name: Glute and Adductor Integration
blocks.instructions: Squeeze the inner thighs to activate the pelvic floor. Everything works together.
movements: Standing Pilates Lunge with Rotation (8 each side)

title: Full Mat Flow
description: The complete classical Pilates mat sequence. Every major movement pattern covered. This is what the method was designed to feel like.

blocks.name: Supine Series
blocks.instructions: Begin every movement from a stable neutral pelvis. Exhale on effort.
movements: Single Leg Circle (5 each direction, each side)

blocks.name: Prone and Side-Lying
blocks.instructions: Keep the core braced in all prone work. The lower abdominals lift the pelvis slightly.
movements: Side-Lying Leg Series (10 each: abduction, circles, kick forward/back)

blocks.name: Finishing
blocks.instructions: Stay connected and intentional to the end.

title: Reformer Fundamentals (Mat Simulation)
description: Reformer-principle exercises done on the mat using resistance bands. All the tension, none of the machine.

blocks.name: Footwork
blocks.instructions: Maintain consistent band tension through the full range. Heels, toes, and turned-out positions.

blocks.name: Upper Body
blocks.instructions: Scapulae stay depressed throughout. No shrugging.

blocks.name: Coordination
blocks.instructions: Breathe into the movement. Curl, extend, lower, return.

title: Advanced Control Series
description: High-demand Pilates control work. Strength, coordination, and breathwork at their limit. This is the session that separates serious practitioners.

blocks.name: Spinal Articulation
blocks.instructions: Every vertebra is accountable. No segment gets a free ride.
movements: Roll Over (6 reps - control the descent)

blocks.name: Strength Series
blocks.instructions: Brace the powerhouse before you move anything. The extremities are levers - the core is the anchor.

blocks.name: Prone Power
blocks.instructions: Pull the navel from the floor throughout. Never relax the abdominals in prone.

blocks.name: Finishing Sequence
blocks.instructions: Carry the quality you built through the whole session into these last movements.

title: Teaser Progression
description: The Teaser is the definitive Pilates test. This session builds every component - hamstring flexibility, hip flexor strength, and spinal articulation - before demanding the full movement.

blocks.name: Preparation
blocks.instructions: Build each component separately before combining them.
movements: Single Leg Stretch into Hold (6 each side)
Supine Leg Extension Hold (30s each side)

blocks.name: Teaser Progressions
blocks.instructions: Progress only when the previous level is controlled. No hip flexor dominance.
movements: Teaser I - One Leg Extended (5 each side)
Teaser II - Both Legs Extended (5 reps)
Teaser III - Arms and Legs Lower Together (4 reps)

blocks.name: Integration
blocks.instructions: String the movements. No rest between exercises in this block.
movements: Roll Up into Teaser (5 full cycles)
Teaser to Rolling Like a Ball (5 transitions)

title: Reformer Advanced Flow (Mat Simulation)
description: Advanced reformer work translated to mat with bands. Long-spine, elephant, and short-spine mechanics recreated with resistance. Demanding and precise.

blocks.name: Long-Spine Mechanics
blocks.instructions: Maintain a flat, neutral spine throughout. No lumbar rounding until explicitly required.
movements: Supine Long Spine Stretch with Band (8 reps)
Hamstring Press Series - Turned Out (10 reps)

blocks.name: Short Spine and Round Back
blocks.instructions: Let the spine round deeply and deliberately. Control the decompression.
movements: Stomach Massage Round Back with Band (10 reps)

blocks.name: Side-Lying Series
blocks.instructions: Keep the waist lifted off the floor. No collapsing into the mat.
movements: Side-Lying Kick Forward and Back (10 each side)

blocks.name: Standing Pilates
blocks.instructions: Ground through all four corners of the foot. The floor is your feedback.

title: Full Classical Sequence
description: The complete classical Pilates mat order, performed at pace with full expression of every movement. One hour of intentional work from the floor to standing.

blocks.name: Supine Foundations
blocks.instructions: The quality of your Hundred sets the tone for the whole session. Do not rush it.
movements: Single Leg Circle (5 each direction, each side)
Single Straight Leg Stretch (10 each side)

blocks.name: Extension Spinal Series
blocks.instructions: Spine Stretch is rest. Use it. Rocker demands full attention.

blocks.name: Prone Series
blocks.instructions: Lift the powerhouse from the mat before raising any limb.

blocks.name: Inversion and Rotation
blocks.instructions: Keep your breath moving through all inversion work.
movements: Side Kick Series (10 each: forward/back, up/down, circle)

blocks.name: Advanced Finishers
blocks.instructions: You have been building to these. Execute them with everything you have left.

blocks.name: Standing Close-Out
blocks.instructions: Carry every principle from the mat into the vertical.

title: Strength and Stretch
description: 60 minutes targeting the strength-flexibility continuum. Loaded stretching, eccentric control, and deep tissue release. The session your body has been asking for.

blocks.name: Loaded Warm-Up
blocks.instructions: Each movement should create a mild stretch under tension. That is the point.
movements: Standing Roll Down with Hold (8 reps, 3s hold at bottom)
Deep Squat with Thoracic Rotation (8 each side)

blocks.name: Hamstring and Hip Flexor
blocks.instructions: Lengthen under load. Never relax the target muscle completely - maintain a gentle eccentric tension.
movements: Supine Hip Flexor Extension (45s each side)
Standing Single Leg Romanian Hinge (8 each side)
Pigeon Pose with Arm Reach (60s each side)

blocks.name: Spinal Loading
blocks.instructions: This is controlled weight bearing through the spine. Move slowly and breathe.
movements: Child's Pose to Swan (10 full cycles)
Side-Lying Banana with Reach (10 each side)

blocks.name: Shoulder and Thoracic
blocks.instructions: Open the anterior shoulder capsule. No aggressive forcing - progressive load only.
movements: Doorway Chest Stretch with Reach (45s each side)

blocks.name: Integration Closing
blocks.instructions: Bring the session to stillness. Hold the last position for as long as it feels productive.

title: The Pilates Athlete
description: Designed for strength athletes who want to move better. Olympic stability demands, rotational power, and the kind of body awareness that makes every other session more effective.

blocks.name: Joint Prep
blocks.instructions: This is not a warmup. It is precise activation of the stabilisers that protect your shoulders, hips, and spine during loaded work.
movements: Shoulder CARs (5 each side - controlled articular rotations)

blocks.name: Unilateral Stability
blocks.instructions: Single-leg and single-arm work exposes asymmetries and corrects them. Note which side is weaker. Address it.
movements: Single-Leg Bridge with Arm Reach (10 each side)
Side Plank with Leg Lift (8 each side)

blocks.name: Overhead Stability
blocks.instructions: The foundation for every snatch and jerk catch. The shoulder must move freely and stabilise powerfully.
movements: Supine Shoulder Flexion with Band (12 reps)
Half-Kneeling Press and Balance (8 each side)
Bear Crawl Hold (3 x 20s - scapular loading)

blocks.name: Rotational Power
blocks.instructions: Decelerate as hard as you accelerate. The rotation is sport. The control is performance.
movements: Pilates Saw with Resistance (10 each side)
Standing Thoracic Rotation with Hold (8 each side)
Seated Rotation into Reach (10 each side)

blocks.name: Breath and Reset
blocks.instructions: Box breathing. 4s in, 4s hold, 4s out, 4s hold. This is performance recovery.

title: The 600m Sting
description: It reads like a chipper... but it's not.

blocks.instructions:
Run 600m. Into: 10 Chest to Bar Pullups, 10 Burpee Box Jump Over, 10 Toes to Bar, 10 Box Jumps. Add 2 reps to each movement until 09:00.

SCORE 1.1 = 600m Run Time
SCORE 1.2 = Reps
movements: Add 2 reps to each movement until 09:00 - score total reps as 1.2

blocks.instructions:
Run 600m. Into: 8 Pullups, 8 Burpee Box Jump Over, 8 Toes to Bar, 8 Box Jumps. Add 2 reps to each movement until 09:00.

SCORE 1.1 = 600m Run Time
SCORE 1.2 = Reps
movements: Add 2 reps to each movement until 09:00 - score total reps as 1.2

blocks.instructions:
Run 600m. Into: 8 American Kettlebell Swings (20kg/12kg), 8 Burpee Box Step Up, 8 Hanging Leg Raises, 8 Box Jumps / Step Up. Add 2 reps to each movement until 09:00.

SCORE 1.1 = 600m Run Time
SCORE 1.2 = Reps
movements: Add 2 reps to each movement until 09:00 - score total reps as 1.2
